_meta.note: Domains realigned to the NACSA Code of Practice Template (18 domains, sections 4.0-21.0) with NCSB v1.4 category cross-references. Previous v2.0 used 15 custom domains; v3.0 adopts the authoritative CoP structure.

governance-leadership.name: Governance and Leadership
governance-leadership.description: Governance structures, leadership commitment, CISO/ICTSO appointment, roles and responsibilities, and director liability awareness under s52. CoP-only domain with no direct NCSB mapping.

cyber-security-policy.name: Cyber Security Policy and Objectives
cyber-security-policy.description: Establishment, alignment, application, review, and communication of the cyber security policy and measurable objectives. NCSB elements: 3.1.1 (Cyber Security Policy, 5 sub-reqs), 3.1.2 (Cyber Security Objectives, 3 sub-reqs).

organisational-development.name: Organisational Development
organisational-development.description: Resources (manpower and budget), authorities and special interest groups (NC4, NACSA, RMP), and cyber security awareness and training. NCSB elements: 3.2.1 (Resources, 4 sub-reqs), 3.2.2 (Authorities, 3 sub-reqs), 3.2.3 (Awareness & Training, 3 sub-reqs).

cyber-security-assurance.name: Cyber Security Assurance
cyber-security-assurance.description: IT Acceptable Use Policy, cyber security audit (biennial, CE submission within 30 days), and supplier relationship security (SCM policy, CSSP licensing per P.U.(A) 221/2024). NCSB elements: 3.3.1 (IT AUP, 3 sub-reqs), 3.3.2 (Audit, 5 sub-reqs), 3.3.3 (Suppliers, 3 sub-reqs).

resource-allocation.name: Resource Allocation and Optimisation
resource-allocation.description: Asset management (inventory, classification, ownership, lifecycle) and capacity management (monitoring, forecasting, performance testing). NCSB elements: 3.4.1 (Asset Management, 4 sub-reqs), 3.4.2 (Capacity Management, 4 sub-reqs).

risk-management.name: Risk Management
risk-management.description: Risk assessment (methodology, best practice alignment, annual conduct per P.U.(A) 219/2024, CE submission, prioritisation) and risk treatment (RTP, approval, implementation, reporting). NCSB elements: 3.5.1 (Risk Assessment, 5 sub-reqs), 3.5.2 (Risk Treatment, 4 sub-reqs).

operational-efficiency.name: Operational Efficiency
operational-efficiency.description: Change management procedures, application, authorisation, and review. NCSB elements: 3.6.1 (Change Management, 4 sub-reqs).

data-security.name: Data Security
data-security.description: Data leakage prevention (DLP), data privacy and protection (PDPA Act 709, OSA Act 88), software and application security (OWASP, SDLC), and secure configuration (hardening, drift detection, logs). NCSB elements: 3.7.1 (DLP, 3 sub-reqs), 3.7.2 (Privacy, 5 sub-reqs), 3.7.3 (AppSec, 4 sub-reqs), 3.7.4 (Config, 3 sub-reqs).

contractual-management.name: Contractual Management
contractual-management.description: Non-disclosure agreements — establishment, signing by all parties, and compliance monitoring. NCSB elements: 3.8.1 (NDA, 3 sub-reqs).

physical-security.name: Physical Security
physical-security.description: Physical security procedures (access control, CCTV, visitor management, emergency response) and security perimeters (zones, monitoring, threat protection). NCSB elements: 3.9.1 (Procedure, 5 sub-reqs), 3.9.2 (Perimeters, 3 sub-reqs).

system-network-security.name: System and Network Security
system-network-security.description: Network security policies and procedures, physical and logical network diagrams, network security controls (firewall, IDS/IPS, NAC), and malware detection and prevention. NCSB elements: 3.10.1 (Policies, 5 sub-reqs), 3.10.2 (Diagrams, 4 sub-reqs), 3.10.3 (Controls, 4 sub-reqs), 3.10.4 (Malware, 3 sub-reqs).

access-control.name: Access Control
access-control.description: Identity and access management (IAM with MFA), segregation of duties, and password management. NCSB elements: 3.11.1 (IAM, 4 sub-reqs), 3.11.2 (SoD, 3 sub-reqs), 3.11.3 (Password, 4 sub-reqs).

technical-vulnerability.name: Technical Vulnerability
technical-vulnerability.description: Vulnerability management procedure (VMP) and vulnerability assessment (VAP) including scanning, evaluation, remediation tracking, and reporting. NCSB elements: 3.12.1 (VMP, 4 sub-reqs), 3.12.2 (VA, 5 sub-reqs).

event-management.name: Cyber Security Event Management
event-management.description: Security monitoring procedure (SMP) — objectives, scope, tools (IDPS/SIEM/endpoint), roles, threat intelligence integration, KPIs (MTTD/MTTR), and review. NCSB elements: 3.13.1 (Event Management, 3 sub-reqs).

incident-management.name: Cyber Security Incident Management
incident-management.description: Cyber security incident management procedure (CSIMP) — lifecycle (Plan/Detect/Respond/Update), CSIRT, 6-hour notification to CE via NC4 portal per P.U.(A) 220/2024, handling, escalation, documentation, and review. NCSB elements: 3.14.1 (Incident Procedure, 3 sub-reqs).

business-continuity.name: Business Continuity Management
business-continuity.description: Business continuity plan (BIA, crisis management, recovery strategies, communication, documentation) and testing and simulation (scenario-based, tabletop, live, red team, reporting). NCSB elements: 3.15.1 (BCP, 3 sub-reqs), 3.15.2 (Testing, 2 sub-reqs).

sector-specific.name: Sector-Specific Requirements
sector-specific.description: Placeholder for sector-specific requirements defined by NCII sector leads in their sector CoPs. Examples include OT security (ICS/SCADA/IoT) for energy, transport, and water sectors, and e-commerce security (PCI-DSS) for banking and trade sectors. CoP-only domain with no NCSB mapping.

monitoring-compliance.name: Monitoring and Compliance
monitoring-compliance.description: Compliance monitoring reports (training %, incident response effectiveness, security control implementation status) and review and continuous improvement (annual CoP review, triggered reviews, action plans). CoP-only domain with no NCSB mapping.
